volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.8.1360 - Section 5.1.1415)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.8.1360
sections.translation: Take care and be honest .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1360b
sections.original: Iam non possum , amisi omnem lubidinem .
sections.translation: I can be so no longer ; I have lost all inclination [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1361
sections.translation: Go , follow them ; don ’ t linger .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1361b
sections.translation: Fare you right well .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1361c
sections.translation: And heartily fare you well .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1362
sections.original: Quaeso memineris , si forte liber fieri occeperim
sections.translation: Prithee , do remember me ; if perchance I should happen to be made free , I ’ ll send the news to you ; don ’ t you forsake me [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1363b
sections.translation: That is not my habit .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1364
sections.original: Cogitato identidem , tibi quam fidelis fuerim .
sections.translation: Consider every now and then how faithful I have been to you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1365
sections.original: si id facies , tum demum scibis , tibi qui bonus sit , qui malus .
sections.translation: If you do that , then at last you ’ ll know who is honest towards you and who dishonest .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1366
sections.translation: I know it ; I have often found that true , as well before as to-day in especial .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1366b
sections.translation: Do you know it ? Aye , and this day I ’ ll make you hereafter say still more how true it is .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1368
sections.original: Vix reprimor quin te manere iubeam .
sections.translation: I can hardly refrain from bidding you to stay .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1368b
sections.translation: Take you care how [*] you do that . They may say that you are a liar and not truthful , that you have no honor ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.1370
sections.original: dicent servorum praeter me esse hic fidelem neminem .
sections.translation: they may say that no one of your slaves is trustworthy except my-self . If , indeed , I thought you could do it with honor , I should advise you . But it cannot be ; take care how you do so .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1372b
sections.translation: Be off ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.1372c
sections.translation: I ’ ll be content then , whatever happens .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1373
sections.translation: Then , fare you well .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1373b
sections.translation: ’ Twere better you should go with a good heart .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1373c
sections.translation: Still , once more , farewell .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1374
sections.original: ante hoc factum hunc sum arbitratus semper servom pessumum :
sections.translation: Before this affair , I had always thought that he was a most rascally servant ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.1375
sections.original: eum fidelem mi esse invenio . cum egomet mecum cogito ,
sections.translation: still , I find that he is faithful to me . When I consider with myself , I have done unwisely in parting with him . I ’ ll go hence at once now to my love here : the door , too , I perceive , makes a noise there .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1378
sections.original: Ne me moneatis , memini ego officium meum ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be advising me ; I remember my duty ; this moment I ’ ll find him . Wherever on earth he may chance to be ,

sections.label: Section 4.9.1380
sections.translation: I ’ ll search him out ; I ’ ll not be sparing of my pains .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1381
sections.original: Me quaerit illic . ibo huic puero obviam .
sections.translation: ’ Tis I he is looking for , I ’ ll go and meet this boy .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1382
sections.original: Ehem , te quaero . salve , vir lepidissime ,
sections.translation: O , I ’ m looking for you ; save you , dearest sir , one loaded by opportunity with her gifts , and whom before all others two Divinities do favour .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1384b
sections.translation: What two ?

sections.label: Section 4.9.1384c
sections.translation: Mars and Venus .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1385
sections.translation: A sprightly boy .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1385b
sections.translation: She entreats that you will go in ; she wishes — she longs for you , and while expecting you , she ’ s dying for you . Do succour one in love . Why do you stay ? Why don ’ t you go in ?

sections.label: Section 4.9.1387b
sections.translation: Well , I ’ ll go .

sections.label: Section 4.9.1388
sections.original: Ipsus illic sese iam impedivit in plagas ;
sections.translation: There has he entangled himself at once in the toils . The snare is prepared : the old gentleman is standing at his post [*]

sections.label: Section 4.9.1390
sections.original: ut adoriatur moechum , qui formast ferox ,
sections.translation: to attack the letcher , who is so boastful of his good looks ; who thinks that , whatever woman sees him , all are in love with him ; whom all , both men and women , detest . Now I will on to the uproar ; I hear a tumult within .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1394
sections.original: Dúcite istum ; sí non sequitur , rápite sublimén foras ,
sections.translation: Bring that fellow along . If he doesn ’ t follow , drag him , lifted on high [*] , out of doors .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1395
sections.original: facite inter terram atque caelum ut sit situs , discindite .
sections.translation: Make him to be between heaven and earth ; cut him in pieces .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1396
sections.translation: By my troth , I do entreat you , Periplecomenus .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1396b
sections.translation: By my troth , you do entreat in vain . Take care , Cario , that that knife of yours is very sharp .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1398
sections.original: Quin iamdudum gestit moecho hóc abdomen adimere ,
sections.translation: Why , it ’ s already longing to rip up the stomach of this letcher . I ’ ll make his entrails hang just as a bauble hangs from a baby ’ s neck .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1400
sections.translation: I ’ m a dead man .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1400b
sections.translation: Not yet ; you say so too soon .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1400c
sections.translation: Shall I have at this fellow now ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1401
sections.translation: Aye ,— but first let him be thrashed with cudgels .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1401b
sections.translation: True , right lustily .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1402
sections.original: Cur es ausus subigitare álienam uxorem , impudens ?
sections.translation: Why have you dared , you disgraceful fellow , to seduce another man ’ s wife ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1403
sections.original: Ita me di ament , ultro ventumst ad me .
sections.translation: So may the Gods bless me , she came to me of her own accord .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1403b
sections.translation: It ’ s a lie . Lay on .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1404
sections.translation: Stay , while I tell —

sections.label: Section 5.1.1404b
sections.translation: Why are you hesitating ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1404c
sections.translation: Will you not let me speak ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1405
sections.translation: Speak , then .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1405b
sections.original: Oratus sum , ad eam ut irem .
sections.translation: I was entreated to come here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1405c
sections.original: Quor ire ausus esausu's ? em tibi .
sections.translation: How did you dare ? There ’ s for you , take that .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1406
sections.translation: O ! O ! I ’ ve had enough . Prithee , now .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1406b
sections.translation: Am I to begin cutting him up at once ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1407
sections.original: Vbi lubet : dispennite hominem divorsum et distendite .
sections.translation: As soon as you like . Stretch the fellow out , and spread out his pinions [*] in opposite ways .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1408
sections.original: Obsecro hercle te , ut mea verba aúdias prius quam secat .
sections.translation: By heavens , prithee , do hear my words before he cuts me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1409
sections.translation: Speak before you ’ re made of no sex .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1409b
sections.original: Non volui nec factum est : viduam hercle esse censui ,
sections.translation: I supposed that she was a widow ; and so her maid , who was her go-between , informed me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1411
sections.original: Iura te non nociturum esse hómini de hac re nemini ,
sections.translation: Now take an oath that you won ’ t injure any person for this affair , because you have been beaten here today , or shall be beaten hereafter , if we let you go safe hence , you dear little grandson of Venus [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1414
sections.original: Iuro per Iovem et Mavortem , me nociturum nemini ,
sections.translation: I swear by Dione [*] and Mars that I will hurt no one

sections.label: Section 5.1.1415
sections.original: quod ego hic hodie vapularim , iureque id factum arbitror ;
sections.translation: because I have been beaten here this day ; and I think that it was rightfully done ; and if I don ’ t go hence further injured , I am rightly punished for the offence .
